Worker appends discovered sub-tasks as structured items in the parent's notes or a new discovered-subtask event. The parent becomes the durable record; future runs or the operator can decompose.\n\n(b) Surface via result. Agent emits a structured discovered_subtasks: [...] field in result.json; the work / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.\n\n(c) Create beads in-band (current behavior). Worker calls ddx bead create directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue on over-decomposition.\n\nToday's behavior is (c) but undocumented. One worker spawned 11 read-coverage children in a single run (ddx-44236615), all P0, without operator review. The work may be valid but the pattern needs a documented position.\n\nAdditionally, 22 existing beads have malformed IDs of the form .execute-bead-wt-\u003cparent\u003e-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003crandom\u003e-\u003chex\u003e (caused by the layer-1 bug fixed in ddx-7eab13a6). A mass-repair or close+refile decision should be made once the design position is set.\n\nScope: pick (a), (b), or (c) and document in a spec amendment (FEAT-006 or FEAT-010 if applicable); record the rationale. After ~20 minutes the session log was 33 MB (8461+ events, 249 messages) and stuck in:\n\n {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n ...\n\n3. Parent ddx process in __futex_wait. Had to SIGTERM the parent (PID 4141811) and manually fast-forward-merge the worktree commit to land the work.\n\n4. A1 (previous bead) ran the same runner/route-target and terminated correctly in ~14 min with identical-looking commit shape. The difference was bead size: A2's output was a single 633-line Rust file, vs A1's 350-line SQL + 290-line test file. A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it.\n\n## Expected\n\n- After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate\n- Compaction should either succeed (messages_after \u003c messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely\n- If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin\n\n## Scope\n\n- Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status\n- Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn\n- Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls\n- Add a watchdog at the work level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget\n\n## Evidence\n\n- Session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)\n- Rescued commit: 91d4bdb on axon master\n- Parent process: PID 4141811 (killed)\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] Compaction retry loop is bounded and fails structurally\n- [ ] Post-commit termination path is deterministic\n- [ ] Watchdog detects and terminates stuck sessions with a preserved-commit outcome\n- [ ] Regression test reproduces the stuck state (seeded session with large no-op tool calls)","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:stability"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-25a41f51","title":"bug: embedded agent stalls in post-commit compaction loop","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"The embedded 'agent' runner can complete a task successfully (commit produced, tests pass) but then fail to terminate, getting stuck in a compaction retry loop with compaction always failing. The parent work never observes task_succeeded and hangs forever.\n\n## Observed\n\nRunning 'ddx agent work --once --local --runner agent --route-target qwen/qwen3-coder-next' against axon-43ed769b (axon auth-core-types bead):\n\n1. qwen3-coder-next ran the task correctly, producing commit 91d4bdb with 636 LOC of types + 109 green tests + clippy clean. The commit was present in the worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-43ed769b-20260415T005652-ebe7a16a.\n\n2. Instead of terminating, the agent kept generating events. After ~20 minutes the session log was 33 MB (8461+ events, 249 messages) and stuck in:\n\n {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n ...\n\n3. Parent ddx process in __futex_wait. Had to SIGTERM the parent (PID 4141811) and manually fast-forward-merge the worktree commit to land the work.\n\n4. A1 (previous bead) ran the same runner/route-target and terminated correctly in ~14 min with identical-looking commit shape. The difference was bead size: A2's output was a single 633-line Rust file, vs A1's 350-line SQL + 290-line test file. A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it.\n\n## Expected\n\n- After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate\n- Compaction should either succeed (messages_after \u003c messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely\n- If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin\n\n## Scope\n\n- Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status\n- Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn\n- Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls\n- Add a watchdog at the work level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget\n\n## Evidence\n\n- Session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)\n- Rescued commit: 91d4bdb on axon master\n- Parent process: PID 4141811 (killed)\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] Compaction retry loop is bounded and fails structurally\n- [ ] Post-commit termination path is deterministic\n- [ ] Watchdog detects and terminates stuck sessions with a preserved-commit outcome\n- [ ] Regression test reproduces the stuck state (seeded session with large no-op tool calls)","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:stability"]}} Local --local workers are not tracked by the server at all.\n\nThis bead adds ddx agent workers as a top-level CLI subcommand. It must not depend on worktrunk (wt) or any external tool.\n\n## Required changes\n\n### 1. New command: ddx agent workers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)\nRegister alongside the existing ddx agent log command. Flags:\n --json emit raw JSON array\n --watch re-render every 2s (like watch -n2) until Ctrl-C\n --project filter to a specific project root (default: CWD project)\n\n### 2. Data sources (two paths)\nA. Server-submitted workers: GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/agent/workers — deserialize []WorkerRecord from the server. Already implemented in cli/internal/server/workers.go List() / HTTP handler.\nB. Local --local workers: the server does not track these. Detect them by:\n 1. Reading .ddx/workers/*/status.json files in the project root (server writes these even for local workers when they self-register — confirm in workers.go; if not, fall back to scanning active execute-bead worktrees)\n 2. Scanning .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories (from ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go line 403) and reading the manifest.json inside each to extract bead_id, attempt_id, runner, route-target, started_at\n\n### 3. Local worker detection via manifest scan\nFor each directory matching .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*:\n - Read .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/manifest.json (path encoded in worktree name suffix)\n - Check if worktree is still active (git worktree list --porcelain)\n - Read work-heartbeat-at from bead.Extra (requires ddx-7651506a) if available\n - Derive synthetic WorkerRecord from manifest fields\n\n### 4. Table output (default)\nColumns: STATE | BEAD | TITLE (truncated 40 chars) | RUNNER | ROUTE-TARGET | ELAPSED | ATTEMPTS\n- STATE: running (green), exited (dim), failed (red)\n- ELAPSED: human-readable (e.g., 4m32s)\n- If current bead title unavailable (local worker pre-manifest), show bead ID\n\n### 5. Merge and deduplicate\nWhen both server and manifest sources are available, deduplicate by worker_id or attempt_id. Server record takes precedence over manifest-derived record for the same worker.\n\n## Key files\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — add newAgentWorkersCommand(), register in newAgentCommand()\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go — WorkerRecord struct (lines 31-54), List API\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant (line 403), manifest shape (lines 726-771)\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry Correlation map (bead_id field)","acceptance":"ddx agent workers prints a table with STATE, BEAD ID, TITLE, RUNNER, ROUTE-TARGET, ELAPSED, ATTEMPTS columns. No dependency on worktrunk or any external binary beyond git.","labels":["ux","work","observability","cli"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-9060c8b2","title":"Add ddx agent workers command — native live worker status with current bead","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"There is no CLI command to see which workers are running and which bead each is executing. The WorkerManager in cli/internal/server/workers.go already tracks this (WorkerRecord.CurrentBead at line 51, WorkerRecord.State, WorkerRecord.Runner, WorkerRecord.Route-target, WorkerRecord.Attempts/Successes/Failures) but it is only accessible through the server API. Local --local workers are not tracked by the server at all.\n\nThis bead adds ddx agent workers as a top-level CLI subcommand. It must not depend on worktrunk (wt) or any external tool.\n\n## Required changes\n\n### 1. New command: ddx agent workers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)\nRegister alongside the existing ddx agent log command. Flags:\n --json emit raw JSON array\n --watch re-render every 2s (like watch -n2) until Ctrl-C\n --project filter to a specific project root (default: CWD project)\n\n### 2. Data sources (two paths)\nA. Server-submitted workers: GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/agent/workers — deserialize []WorkerRecord from the server. Already implemented in cli/internal/server/workers.go List() / HTTP handler.\nB. Local --local workers: the server does not track these. Detect them by:\n 1. Reading .ddx/workers/*/status.json files in the project root (server writes these even for local workers when they self-register — confirm in workers.go; if not, fall back to scanning active execute-bead worktrees)\n 2. Scanning .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories (from ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go line 403) and reading the manifest.json inside each to extract bead_id, attempt_id, runner, route-target, started_at\n\n### 3. Local worker detection via manifest scan\nFor each directory matching .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*:\n - Read .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/manifest.json (path encoded in worktree name suffix)\n - Check if worktree is still active (git worktree list --porcelain)\n - Read work-heartbeat-at from bead.Extra (requires ddx-7651506a) if available\n - Derive synthetic WorkerRecord from manifest fields\n\n### 4. Table output (default)\nColumns: STATE | BEAD | TITLE (truncated 40 chars) | RUNNER | ROUTE-TARGET | ELAPSED | ATTEMPTS\n- STATE: running (green), exited (dim), failed (red)\n- ELAPSED: human-readable (e.g., 4m32s)\n- If current bead title unavailable (local worker pre-manifest), show bead ID\n\n### 5. Merge and deduplicate\nWhen both server and manifest sources are available, deduplicate by worker_id or attempt_id. Server record takes precedence over manifest-derived record for the same worker.\n\n## Key files\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — add newAgentWorkersCommand(), register in newAgentCommand()\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go — WorkerRecord struct (lines 31-54), List API\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant (line 403), manifest shape (lines 726-771)\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry Correlation map (bead_id field)","acceptance":"ddx agent workers prints a table with STATE, BEAD ID, TITLE, RUNNER, ROUTE-TARGET, ELAPSED, ATTEMPTS columns. Mutation returns success with a worker id. Navigating to `/nodes/.../projects/\u003cid\u003e/workers` shows no workers — an empty table.\n\nVerified on disk: the worker record exists and is running.\n\n```\n$ cat .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T205846-f23f/status.json\n{\n \"id\": \"worker-20260422T205846-f23f\",\n \"kind\": \"work\",\n \"state\": \"running\",\n \"project_root\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\",\n ...\n}\n```\n\nSo the dispatch worked, but the per-project workers list filters it out.\n\n## Root cause\n\n`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:156` in `GetWorkersGraphQL`:\n\n```go\nif projectID != \"\" \u0026\u0026 rec.ProjectRoot != projectID {\n continue\n}\n```\n\n- `projectID` is the GraphQL argument — a project **id** like `proj-96d7ea83`.\n- `rec.ProjectRoot` is the worker record field — a project **path** like `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx`.\n\nThese are different representations and never match. With any non-empty `projectID`, every worker is filtered out.\n\nThe global `workers` resolver (`resolver_agent.go:9`) passes `\"\"` and works correctly. Only the per-project path (`workersByProject` → `GetWorkersGraphQL(projectID)`) is broken. The user's flow (dispatch from project home → navigate to project workers) hits exactly this path.\n\nNote: `GetBeadSnapshots` (state_graphql.go:49-105) handles the same concept correctly by iterating `s.Projects` and filtering on `proj.ID != projectID` — comparing id to id. The worker resolver does not use that pattern.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nTwo options; prefer (1) for minimal change, (2) for long-term clarity.\n\n### Option 1 — Resolve id → path at the filter site\n\n```go\nexpectedPath := \"\"\nif projectID != \"\" {\n proj, ok := s.GetProjectByID(projectID)\n if !ok {\n return nil // unknown project id → no workers\n }\n expectedPath = proj.Path\n}\n...\nif expectedPath != \"\" \u0026\u0026 rec.ProjectRoot != expectedPath {\n continue\n}\n```\n\n### Option 2 — Store project id on the worker record\n\nAdd `ProjectID string` to `WorkerRecord`. Populate at creation time in `WorkerManager.StartExecuteLoop` via `s.GetProjectByPath(effectiveRoot)`. Filter on `rec.ProjectID != projectID`. Cleaner — the worker record carries its own identity and the query doesn't depend on runtime resolution. Requires a tiny migration (for older records missing the field, fall back to path-resolve as in Option 1).\n\nRecommend Option 2. It removes the representation mismatch for good and is cheap.\n\n## Scope note\n\nThis is a one-line functional bug with outsized impact: it's the reason the Workers page looks broken whenever used as the dashboard intends. Prioritizing P1.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Workers pane lifecycle controls (`ddx-69789664`).\n- Cross-project workers view — the global `workers` query already handles that.","acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, clicking Drain queue on a project home page starts a worker that I can immediately see in that project's Workers view, with its state and current bead updating live.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Root-cause fix.** The `workersByProject(projectID:)` resolver returns all workers whose target project matches the requested id. Both Option 1 (id→path resolution) and Option 2 (store project id on record) satisfy this; implementation chooses one with a one-line justification in the PR description.\n\n2. **Regression test — project-scoped query.**\n - Seed a ServerState with two registered projects (different paths and ids).\n - Start one worker for each project.\n - `workersByProject(projectID: \u003cid-1\u003e)` returns exactly worker 1.\n - `workersByProject(projectID: \u003cid-2\u003e)` returns exactly worker 2.\n - `workers` (global) returns both.\n - Unknown project id returns empty list, not an error.\n\n3. **Regression test — backward compat.**\n - A worker record on disk without a `ProjectID` field (simulating a pre-migration record) is correctly matched by the path-resolution fallback. Only required if Option 2 is chosen.\n\n4. **End-to-end validation.**\n - Playwright: navigate to project home, click Drain, confirm, wait 2s, navigate to Workers tab. The codex reviewer's BLOCK rationale ('change set only adds execution metadata files') becomes redundant — the runner catches this case before the reviewer is even invoked, saving a redundant review pass and its cost.\n\nAC3. The execute-bead prompt template is updated so the rationale-on-incomplete-work instruction is harder to skip — ideally a tool the agent must invoke (e.g. SubmitNoChangesRationale tool) rather than 'write a file and exit'.\n\nAC4. When the agent did run Edit tool calls but the worktree has no commit, the runner logs a warning naming each file the agent edited that is now uncommitted, so operators can recover the work or diagnose the silent-commit-failure.\n\nAC5. Result is cached for process lifetime. ./docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md:63:6. **Context rot is real; bounded context execution is the response.** LLM output quality decays as a single context window fills — well before the hard token limit. Long-running agent sessions accumulate transcript, tool noise, and failed attempts that compete with the original instructions; the agent at hour one is not the agent at minute one. The structural fix is **bounded context execution** — also known as the **Ralph loop**: every unit of work runs in a fresh, narrowly-scoped context against an explicit contract (a bead with acceptance criteria), with persistent state landing on disk as evidence rather than carried forward as transcript. DDx implements this with `ddx try` (single bounded attempt in an isolated worktree) and `ddx work` (queue drain that re-enters the loop with a fresh context per bead). Each check is one YAML file under ./cli/internal/checks/protocol.go:11:// DIFF_HEAD — current worktree HEAD ./cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":"\u003cexecute-bead\u003e\n \u003cbead id=\"axon-dccbdf73\"\u003e\n \u003ctitle\u003eauth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension\u003c/title\u003e\n \u003cdescription\u003e\nImplement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What you're building\n\nAn axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope — exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.\n\nAdditionally:\n\n- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.\n- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.\n- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.\n- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.\n- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.\n\n### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`\n\nBuild a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:\n- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`\n- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: \"orders\", ops: [read]}]` → 200\n- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`\n- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`\n\n### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`\n\n**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.\n\n## Crate dependencies you will need\n\nAdd to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:\n```toml\njsonwebtoken = \"9\"\n```\n\nEverything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.\n\n## Context you must know about\n\n### A1 landed: schema\n- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.\n- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.\n- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.\n\n### A2 landed: types\n- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).\n- `parse_claims(\u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.\n- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.\n- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.\n\n### Files you should NOT touch\n- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.\n- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.\n\n## Quality bar\n\nYou are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher power class. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued.\n\nSpecifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. Fix the issues first.\n \u003c/description\u003e\n \u003cacceptance\u003e\n- [ ] crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs exists and compiles\n- [ ] JwtIssuer can sign and verify a JwtClaims round-trip (HS256)\n- [ ] Middleware implements the 8-step verification order\n- [ ] Every row of the ADR-018 §4 failure table has a matching unit test asserting (status, error_code)\n- [ ] Revocation cache invalidation test passes without sleep\n- [ ] Clock skew test passes at 30s boundary (both directions)\n- [ ] Integration test with a dummy protected route covers the 4 golden paths\n- [ ] is_jti_revoked wired into memory/sqlite/postgres storage adapters (trait + 3 impls)\n- [ ] get_user and get_tenant_member wired similarly\n- [ ] ResolvedIdentity lives in axon-core and is installed into the axum request extension\n- [ ] cargo test -p axon-server green\n- [ ] cargo test -p axon-storage green (new trait methods added)\n- [ ] cargo test -p axon-core green (new helpers added)\n- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n \u003c/acceptance\u003e\n \u003clabels\u003ehelix, area:auth, spec:ADR-018, phase:middleware\u003c/labels\u003e\n \u003cmetadata parent=\"axon-b170a173\" base-rev=\"HEAD\" bundle=\".ddx/executions/b1-enhanced-manual\"/\u003e\n \u003c/bead\u003e\n\n \u003cgoverning\u003e\n \u003cref id=\"ADR-018\" path=\"docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-018-tenant-user-credential-model.md\"\u003eSections 4 (Credentials) and 4 Verification Order — normative for everything in this bead\u003c/ref\u003e\n \u003cref id=\"FEAT-012\" path=\"docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-012-authorization.md\"\u003eCredentials (JWT, V5) and Identity (Authentication) sections\u003c/ref\u003e\n \u003c/governing\u003e\n\n \u003cinstructions\u003e\nYou are a coding agent implementing a single bead inside an isolated git worktree. Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! # Authentication\\n4\\t//!\\n5\\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n6\\t//! injected as a typed extension. Three modes are supported:\\n7\\t//!\\n8\\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n9\\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n10\\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n11\\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n12\\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n15\\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n16\\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n17\\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n18\\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n21\\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n22\\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n23\\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n24\\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n25\\t//!\\n26\\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n27\\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`. If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n28\\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n29\\t//!\\n30\\t//! # Request flow\\n31\\t//!\\n32\\t//! ```text\\n33\\t//! TCP accept\\n34\\t//! └─ authenticate_http_request (gateway.rs middleware)\\n35\\t//! ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer → Identity\\n36\\t//! └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n37\\t//! └─ route handler\\n38\\t//! ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n39\\t//! ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n40\\t//! └─ handler logic\\n41\\t//! ```\\n42\\t//!\\n43\\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\\n44\\tpub mod actor_scope;\\n45\\tpub mod auth;\\n46\\tmod collection_listing;\\n47\\tpub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\\n48\\tpub We apply a conservative subset of that\\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n if name.is_empty() {\\n return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\\n \\\"database name must not be empty\\\".to_owned(),\\n ));\\n }\\n let first = name.chars().next().expect(\\\"non-empty checked above\\\");\\n if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() \u0026\u0026 first != '_' {\\n return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n \\\"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\\\"\\n )));\\n }\\n if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\\n return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n \\\"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\\\"\\n )));\\n }\\n Ok(())\\n}\\n\\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\\n/// `dbname` key.\\n///\\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database. We produce a\\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, tenant_db_name: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n let target = format!(\\\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\\\");\\n if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgres://\\\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgresql://\\\") {\\n // URL format: replace path component.\\n // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\\n if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\\\"://\\\") {\\n let after_scheme = \u0026superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\\n // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\\n if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\\n let scheme_authority = \u0026superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\\n let rest = \u0026after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\\n // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\\n let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\\n \u0026rest[q..]\\n } else {\\n \\\"\\\"\\n };\\n return format!(\\\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\\\");\\n }\\n // No path: append the target database.\\n return format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\");\\n }\\n format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\")\\n } else {\\n // Keyword–value format. Replace existing dbname= or append it.\\n let mut parts: Vec\u003cString\u003e = superadmin_dsn\\n .split_whitespace()\\n .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\\\"dbname=\\\"))\\n .map(str::to_owned)\\n .collect();\\n parts.push(format!(\\\"dbname={target}\\\"));\\n parts.join(\\\" \\\")\\n }\\n}\\n\\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\\n/// superadmin connection.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\\n/// Returns name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n .enable_all()\\n .build()\\n .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n rt.block_on(async {\\n let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n .await\\n WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n )\\n .await\\n .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n if exists {\\n return transaction that execute() would open.\\n client\\n .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"CREATE DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n .await\\n .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n Ok(())\\n })\\n}\\n\\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges to execute\\n/// `DROP DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// # skip_postgres_test(test_name: \u0026str, reason: \u0026str) {\\n tracing::warn!(test = test_name, reason, \\\"skipping PostgreSQL storage test\\\");\\n }\\n\\n fn database_or_skip(test_name: JWT-based auth pipeline: sign, verify, and enforce credentials (ADR-018 §4).\\n//!\\n//! # Verification order\\n//!\\n//! 1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` → `Unauthenticated` if missing.\\n//! 2. Parse + verify HS256 signature → `CredentialInvalid` / `CredentialMalformed`.\\n//! 3. Check `iss` against issuer ID → `CredentialForeignIssuer`.\\n//! 4. Check `exp`/`nbf` with ±30 s skew → `CredentialExpired` / `CredentialNotYetValid`.\\n//! 5. Check `jti` revocation (cache → storage) → `CredentialRevoked`.\\n//! 6. Compare `aud` to URL tenant → `CredentialWrongTenant`.\\n//! 7. Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\\n//!\\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\\n//! On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\\n\\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::extract::State;\\nuse axum::middleware::Next;\\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\\nuse http::{HeaderMap, Method, StatusCode};\\nuse jsonwebtoken::{Algorithm, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, Validation, decode, encode};\\nuse serde_json::json;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{AuthError, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, UserId};\\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\\npub struct JwtIssuer {\\n secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e,\\n /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\\n pub issuer_id: String,\\n}\\n\\nimpl JwtIssuer {\\n /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\\n pub fn new(secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e, issuer_id: String) -\u003e Self {\\n Self { secret, issuer_id }\\n }\\n\\n /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\\n pub fn issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e {\\n encode(\\n \u0026Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\\n claims,\\n \u0026EncodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret),\\n )\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n }\\n\\n /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\\n ///\\n /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\\n /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\\n pub fn verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e {\\n let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\\n validation.validate_exp = false;\\n validation.validate_nbf = false;\\n validation.validate_aud = false;\\n validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\\n\\n decode::\u003cJwtClaims\u003e(token, \u0026DecodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret), \u0026validation)\\n .map(|td| td.claims)\\n .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\\n jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature =\u003e AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\\n jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n _ =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n })\\n }\\n}\\n\\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\\n///\\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\\n#[derive(Default)]\\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n inner: RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n /// Create an empty cache.\\n pub fn new() -\u003e Self {\\n Self::default()\\n }\\n\\n /// Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\\n pub fn contains(\u0026self, jti: \u0026Uuid) -\u003e bool {\\n self.inner\\n .read()\\n .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\\n .unwrap_or(false)\\n }\\n\\n /// Add `jti` to the cache.\\n pub fn insert(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) {\\n if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\\n g.insert(jti);\\n }\\n }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\\n///\\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to `axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state`.\\n#[derive(Clone)]\\npub struct AuthPipelineState {\\n /// Token issuer / verifier.\\n pub issuer: Arc\u003cJwtIssuer\u003e,\\n /// Fast in-process revocation check.\\n pub revocation_cache: Arc\u003cInMemoryRevocationCache\u003e,\\n /// Authoritative storage for user, membership, and revocation data.\\n pub storage: Arc\u003cMutex\u003cBox\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl AuthPipelineState {\\n /// Convenience constructor.\\n pub fn new(issuer: JwtIssuer, storage: Box\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e) -\u003e Self {\\n Self {\\n issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n }\\n }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 §4 JWT verification order.\\n///\\n/// Install with:\\n/// ```ignore\\n/// Router::new()\\n/// .route(\\\"/tenants/:t/databases/:db/…\\\", …)\\n/// .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n/// Arc::new(state),\\n/// jwt_verify_layer,\\n/// ))\\n/// ```\\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\\n State(state): State\u003cArc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e\u003e,\\n request: axum::extract::Request,\\n next: Next,\\n) -\u003e Response {\\n let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\\n\\n // Skip middleware for paths that are not data-plane paths.\\n let Some((tenant, database)) = extract_tenant_database(\u0026path) else {\\n return next.run(request).await;\\n };\\n\\n let tenant = tenant.to_string();\\n let database = database.to_string();\\n let method = request.method().clone();\\n let headers = request.headers().clone();\\n\\n match verify_request(\u0026state, \u0026headers, \u0026method, \u0026tenant, \u0026database) {\\n Ok(identity) =\u003e {\\n let mut request = request;\\n request.extensions_mut().insert(identity);\\n next.run(request).await\\n }\\n Err(err) =\u003e AuthErrorResponse(err).into_response(),\\n }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Core verification logic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfn verify_request(\\n state: \u0026AuthPipelineState,\\n headers: \u0026HeaderMap,\\n method: \u0026Method,\\n tenant: \u0026str,\\n database: \u0026str,\\n) -\u003e Result\u003cResolvedIdentity, AuthError\u003e {\\n // Step 1: Extract Bearer token.\\n let token = extract_bearer_token(headers)?;\\n\\n // Step 2: Verify JWT signature and structure.\\n let claims = state.issuer.verify(token)?;\\n\\n // Step 3: Check iss.\\n if claims.iss != state.issuer.issuer_id {\\n return Err(AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer);\\n }\\n\\n // Step 4: Check exp/nbf with 30 s skew.\\n let now = SystemTime::now()\\n .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n .unwrap_or_default()\\n .as_secs();\\n const SKEW: u64 = 30;\\n // Expired: exp + skew \u003c now ↔ expired more than `skew` seconds ago\\n if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) \u003c now {\\n return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\\n }\\n // Not yet valid: nbf \u003e now + skew ↔ valid more than `skew` seconds from now\\n if claims.nbf \u003e now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\\n return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\\n }\\n\\n // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\\n let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\\n .jti\\n .parse()\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n if state.revocation_cache.contains(\u0026jti_uuid) {\\n return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n }\\n {\\n let storage = state\\n .storage\\n .lock()\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n if storage\\n .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\\n {\\n // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\\n drop(storage);\\n state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\\n return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n }\\n }\\n\\n // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\\n if claims.aud != tenant {\\n return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\\n }\\n\\n // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\\n let user_id = UserId::new(\u0026claims.sub);\\n let user = {\\n let storage = state\\n .storage\\n .lock()\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n storage\\n .get_user(user_id.clone())\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n };\\n if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\\n return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\\n }\\n\\n // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\\n let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\\n {\\n let storage = state\\n .storage\\n .lock()\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n storage\\n .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\\n .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\\n }\\n\\n // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\\n let granted_db = claims\\n .grants\\n .find_database(database)\\n .ok_or(AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted)?;\\n\\n let required_op = if method == Method::GET {\\n Op::Read\\n } else {\\n Op::Write\\n };\\n if !granted_db.has_op(required_op) {\\n return Err(AuthError::OpNotGranted);\\n }\\n\\n Ok(ResolvedIdentity {\\n user_id,\\n tenant_id,\\n grants: claims.grants,\\n })\\n}\\n\\n// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Extract the `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` value from the `Authorization` header.\\nfn extract_bearer_token(headers: \u0026HeaderMap) -\u003e Result\u003c\u0026str, AuthError\u003e {\\n let auth_header = headers\\n .get(http::header::AUTHORIZATION)\\n .ok_or(AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n\\n let auth_str = auth_header\\n .to_str()\\n .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n\\n auth_str\\n .strip_prefix(\\\"Bearer \\\")\\n .ok_or(AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n}\\n\\n/// Extract `(tenant, database)` from a data-plane URL path.\\n///\\n/// Returns `Some((tenant, database))` for paths matching\\n/// `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/…`, `None` otherwise.\\n/// Non-data-plane paths skip the middleware entirely.\\npub fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e {\\n let path = path.strip_prefix('/')?;\\n let mut parts = path.splitn(5, '/');\\n match (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\\n (Some(\\\"tenants\\\"), Some(tenant), Some(\\\"databases\\\"), Some(rest))\\n if !tenant.is_empty() \u0026\u0026 !rest.is_empty() =\u003e\\n {\\n let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\\n if database.is_empty() {\\n None\\n } else {\\n Some((tenant, database))\\n }\\n }\\n _ =\u003e None,\\n }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthError → axum Response ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Newtype wrapper that lets us implement `IntoResponse` for `AuthError` in\\n/// this crate (orphan-rule: neither `IntoResponse` nor `AuthError` is local).\\npub struct AuthErrorResponse(pub AuthError);\\n\\nimpl IntoResponse for AuthErrorResponse {\\n fn into_response(self) -\u003e Response {\\n let status = StatusCode::from_u16(self.0.status_code())\\n .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\\n let body = json!({\\n \\\"error\\\": {\\n \\\"code\\\": self.0.error_code(),\\n \\\"message\\\": self.0.to_string()\\n }\\n });\\n (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\\n }\\n}\\n\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"stop_details\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":1,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":283,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":118515,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":283},\"output_tokens\":3,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"},\"context_management\":null},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"6e2eb73d-c717-4746-9980-0fe22b34a94b\"}\n{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01Rv6VWw33HurET6YfGKMM4k\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"File GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\n//!\\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\\n\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::body::Body;\\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\\nuse axum::routing::get;\\nuse axum::Router;\\nuse http::Request;\\nuse serde_json::Value;\\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{\\n GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\\n};\\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\\n\\nconst ISSUER: \u0026str = \\\"integration-issuer\\\";\\nconst TENANT: \u0026str = \\\"acme\\\";\\nconst DATABASE: \u0026str = \\\"orders\\\";\\nconst USER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"u-integration-01\\\";\\nconst SECRET: \u0026[u8] = b\\\"integration-test-secret\\\";\\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: \u0026str = \\\"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\\";\\n\\nfn now_secs() -\u003e u64 {\\n SystemTime::now()\\n .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n .unwrap_or_default()\\n .as_secs()\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n storage.insert_user(User {\\n id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n display_name: \\\"Integration User\\\".to_string(),\\n email: None,\\n created_at_ms: 0,\\n suspended_at_ms: None,\\n });\\n storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n role: TenantRole::Write,\\n });\\n Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\\n revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\\n Box::new(storage) as Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\\n )),\\n })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e) -\u003e Router {\\n Router::new()\\n .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\\n .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n state,\\n jwt_verify_layer,\\n ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, Value) {\\n let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service should not fail\\\");\\n let status = resp.status();\\n let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n .await\\n .unwrap_or_default();\\n let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn bearer(token: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn valid_token(issuer: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n iat: now,\\n nbf: now,\\n exp: now + 3600,\\n grants,\\n };\\n issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn orders_read_grants() -\u003e directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\\n\\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\\n```\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n```bash\\nhelix commit hx-abc123 # commit, gate, push, close issue\\nhelix commit # commit and gate without tracker update\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01P2d4aV4hoZu9dRSEoGCXwm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"Now I'll perform the helix commit following the skill instructions. data.text: I’ve located the main surfaces: cleanup code already exists under `cli/internal/agent/*`, and startup/doctor coverage lives under `cli/cmd/*`. Next I’m reading those paths together with the existing startup cleanup tests to see what this child bead already covers and what is still missing.

data.output:
package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"runtime"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorktrees(t *testing.T) {
	if runtime.GOOS == "windows" {
		t.Skip("process-liveness startup cleanup test is unix-oriented")
	}

	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupWorkStartupCleanupProject(t)
	t.Setenv("DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE", "1h")

	attemptID := "20260515T010203-deadbeef"
	worktreePath := createRegisteredStartupWorktree(t, projectRoot, tempRoot, "ddx-startup-stale", attemptID)
	old := time.Now().Add(-2 * time.Hour).UTC()
	writeStartupRunState(t, projectRoot, "ddx-startup-stale", attemptID, worktreePath, deadProcessPID(t), old)
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(worktreePath, old, old))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "--json", "--once", "--project", projectRoot)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var res struct {
		NoReadyWork bool `json:"no_ready_work"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &res))
	assert.True(t, res.NoReadyWork)
	assert.NoFileExists(t, worktreePath)
	assert.NotContains(t, runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "worktree", "list", "--porcelain"), worktreePath)
}

func TestWorkStartup_PreservesLiveWorktrees(t *testing.T) {
	if runtime.GOOS == "windows" {
		t.Skip("process-liveness startup cleanup test is unix-oriented")
	}

	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupWorkStartupCleanupProject(t)
	t.Setenv("DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE", "1h")

	attemptID := "20260515T020304-feedface"
	worktreePath := createRegisteredStartupWorktree(t, projectRoot, tempRoot, "ddx-startup-live", attemptID)
	old := time.Now().Add(-2 * time.Hour).UTC()
	live := startLongLivedProcess(t)
	writeStartupRunState(t, projectRoot, "ddx-startup-live", attemptID, worktreePath, live.Process.Pid, old)
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(worktreePath, old, old))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "--json", "--once", "--project", projectRoot)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var res struct {
		NoReadyWork bool `json:"no_ready_work"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &res))
	assert.True(t, res.NoReadyWork)
	assert.DirExists(t, worktreePath)
	assert.Contains(t, runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "worktree", "list", "--porcelain"), worktreePath)
}

func setupWorkStartupCleanupProject(t *testing.T) (string, string) {
	t.Helper()

	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "init", "-b", "main")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "add", "seed.txt")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed work startup cleanup repo")
	return projectRoot, tempRoot
}

func createRegisteredStartupWorktree(t *testing.T, projectRoot, tempRoot, beadID, attemptID string) string {
	t.Helper()

	worktreePath := filepath.Join(tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+beadID+"-"+attemptID)
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "worktree", "add", "--detach", worktreePath, "HEAD")
	t.Cleanup(func() {
		_ = exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "worktree", "remove", "--force", worktreePath).Run()
	})
	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(worktreePath, agent.ExecutionCleanupMetadata{
		ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
		BeadID:       beadID,
		AttemptID:    attemptID,
		WorktreePath: worktreePath,
		Registered:   true,
	}))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(worktreePath, "scratch.txt"), []byte("payload\n"), 0o644))
	return worktreePath
}

func writeStartupRunState(t *testing.T, projectRoot, beadID, attemptID, worktreePath string, pid int, refreshedAt time.Time) {
	t.Helper()

	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteRunState(projectRoot, agent.RunState{
		BeadID:       beadID,
		AttemptID:    attemptID,
		StartedAt:    refreshedAt.Add(-5 * time.Minute),
		WorktreePath: worktreePath,
		PID:          pid,
		RefreshedAt:  refreshedAt,
		ExpiresAt:    refreshedAt.Add(5 * time.Minute),
	}))
}

func deadProcessPID(t *testing.T) int {
	t.Helper()

	cmd := exec.Command("sh", "-c", "exit 0")
	require.NoError(t, cmd.Start())
	pid := cmd.Process.Pid
	require.NoError(t, cmd.Wait())
	return pid
}

func startLongLivedProcess(t *testing.T) *exec.Cmd {
	t.Helper()

	cmd := exec.Command("sleep", "30")
	require.NoError(t, cmd.Start())
	t.Cleanup(func() {
		if cmd.Process == nil {
			return
		}
		_ = cmd.Process.Kill()
		_, _ = cmd.Process.Wait()
	})
	return cmd
}

data.text: The repo already has a generic cleanup manager and startup worktree tests, so this bead likely fits without decomposition. I’m drilling into how startup cleanup is wired into `work`/`try`, whether worker-dir and evidence retention are already partially implemented, and where `doctor --apply` should hook in.

func setupEvidenceDir(t *testing.T, projectRoot, attemptID string, mtime time.Time) string {
	t.Helper()
	dir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "executions", attemptID)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "result.json"), []byte(`{"status":"success"}`), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(dir, mtime, mtime))
	return dir
}

func TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldDirs(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := setupExecutionCleanupProjectRoot(t)
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()

	oldTime := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -10)
	oldDir := setupEvidenceDir(t, projectRoot, "20260101T000000-deadbeef", oldTime)

	mgr := NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, &executionCleanupTestGitOps{})
	mgr.TempRoot = tempRoot
	mgr.RetainDays = 7

	summary, err := mgr.Cleanup(context.Background())
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.NoDirExists(t, oldDir)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(1), summary.RemovedEvidenceDirs)
	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "removed_evidence_dir"))
}

func TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesActiveDirs(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := setupExecutionCleanupProjectRoot(t)
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()

	oldTime := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -10)
	activeAttemptID := "20260101T000000-aabbccdd"
	activeDir := setupEvidenceDir(t, projectRoot, activeAttemptID, oldTime)

	require.NoError(t, WriteRunState(projectRoot, RunState{
		BeadID:    "ddx-active",
		AttemptID: activeAttemptID,
		StartedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
	}))

	mgr := NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, &executionCleanupTestGitOps{})
	mgr.TempRoot = tempRoot
	mgr.RetainDays = 7

	summary, err := mgr.Cleanup(context.Background())
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.DirExists(t, activeDir)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(0), summary.RemovedEvidenceDirs)
	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "preserved_active_evidence_dir"))
}

func TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldAgentLogs(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := setupExecutionCleanupProjectRoot(t)
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()
	logDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "agent-logs")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(logDir, 0o755))

	oldTime := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -20)
	oldAgent := filepath.Join(logDir, "agent-oldsession.jsonl")
	oldSvc := filepath.Join(logDir, "svc-oldsession.jsonl")
	recentAgent := filepath.Join(logDir, "agent-recentsession.jsonl")
	otherFile := filepath.Join(logDir, "mirror.log")

	for _, p := range []string{oldAgent, oldSvc, recentAgent, otherFile} {
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(p, []byte("data\n"), 0o644))
	}
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(oldAgent, oldTime, oldTime))
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(oldSvc, oldTime, oldTime))

	mgr := NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, &executionCleanupTestGitOps{})
	mgr.TempRoot = tempRoot
	mgr.RetainDays = 7

	summary, err := mgr.Cleanup(context.Background())
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.NoFileExists(t, oldAgent)
	assert.NoFileExists(t, oldSvc)
	assert.FileExists(t, recentAgent)
	assert.FileExists(t, otherFile)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(2), summary.RemovedAgentLogs)
	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "removed_agent_log"))
}

func TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldWorkerDirs(t *testing.T) {

	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(recentWorker, "status.json"), []byte(`{"id":"worker-recent","state":"running"}`), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(oldWorker, oldTime, oldTime))

func TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesActiveItems(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := setupExecutionCleanupProjectRoot(t)
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()
	oldTime := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -20)
	activeSessionID := "activesession123"

	// execution dir: active via run-state
	activeExecID := "20260101T000000-exec-active"
	activeExecDir := setupEvidenceDir(t, projectRoot, activeExecID, oldTime)
	require.NoError(t, WriteRunState(projectRoot, RunState{
		BeadID:    "ddx-active",
		AttemptID: activeExecID,
		SessionID: activeSessionID,
		StartedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
	}))

	// agent log: same session ID as active run-state
	logDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "agent-logs")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(logDir, 0o755))
	activeLog := filepath.Join(logDir, "agent-"+activeSessionID+".jsonl")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(activeLog, []byte("data\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(activeLog, oldTime, oldTime))

	// worker dir: PID 0 but not old enough to be pruned (use recent mtime to preserve)
	workersDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "workers")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(workersDir, 0o755))
	preservedWorker := filepath.Join(workersDir, "worker-preserved")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(preservedWorker, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(preservedWorker, "status.json"), []byte(`{"id":"worker-preserved","state":"running","pid":0}`), 0o644))
	// keep recent mtime so it is not pruned by age

	mgr := NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, &executionCleanupTestGitOps{})
	mgr.TempRoot = tempRoot
	mgr.RetainDays = 7

	summary, err := mgr.Cleanup(context.Background())
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.DirExists(t, activeExecDir)
	assert.FileExists(t, activeLog)
	assert.DirExists(t, preservedWorker)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(0), summary.RemovedEvidenceDirs)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(0), summary.RemovedAgentLogs)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(0), summary.RemovedWorkerDirs)
	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "preserved_active_evidence_dir"))
	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "preserved_active_agent_log"))
}

func TestExecutionCleanup_RecordsCounts(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := setupExecutionCleanupProjectRoot(t)
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()
	oldTime := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -20)

	// 2 old execution dirs
	for _, id := range []string{"20260101T000000-cnt-exec1", "20260101T000001-cnt-exec2"} {
		setupEvidenceDir(t, projectRoot, id, oldTime)
	}

	// 3 old agent logs
	logDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "agent-logs")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(logDir, 0o755))
	for _, name := range []string{"agent-s1.jsonl", "agent-s2.jsonl", "svc-s1.jsonl"} {
		p := filepath.Join(logDir, name)
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(p, []byte("data\n"), 0o644))
		require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(p, oldTime, oldTime))
	}

	// 2 old worker dirs
	workersDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "workers")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(workersDir, 0o755))
	for _, id := range []string{"worker-cnt1", "worker-cnt2"} {
		d := filepath.Join(workersDir, id)
		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(d, 0o755))
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(d, "status.json"), []byte(`{"id":"`+id+`","state":"exited"}`), 0o644))
		require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(d, oldTime, oldTime))
	}

	mgr := NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, &executionCleanupTestGitOps{})
	mgr.TempRoot = tempRoot
	mgr.RetainDays = 7

	summary, err := mgr.Cleanup(context.Background())
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Equal(t, int64(2), summary.RemovedEvidenceDirs)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(3), summary.RemovedAgentLogs)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(2), summary.RemovedWorkerDirs)
}

func hasObservationClass(observations []ExecutionCleanupObservation, class string) bool {
	for _, obs := range observations {
		if obs.Class == class {
			return true
		}
	}
	return false
}

func firstObservationMessage(observations []ExecutionCleanupObservation, class string) string {
	for _, obs := range observations {
		if obs.Class == class {
			return obs.Message
		}
	}
	return ""
}

data.text:
Plan:
1. Add a shared startup housekeeping path for stale worker dirs and execution-evidence retention, then reuse it from `ddx work` startup and `ddx doctor`.
2. Add/adjust tests for startup worker-dir reaping, evidence archiving/deletion behavior, and doctor dry-run vs `--apply`.
3. Run the bead’s required verification (`go test` for the touched packages and `lefthook run pre-commit`), then commit once if green.

func executeWithStdoutCapture(t *testing.T, root *cobra.Command, args ...string) (string, error) {
	t.Helper()

	stdoutR, stdoutW, err := os.Pipe()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	stderrR, stderrW, err := os.Pipe()
	require.NoError(t, err)

	origStdout := os.Stdout
	origStderr := os.Stderr
	os.Stdout = stdoutW
	os.Stderr = stderrW
	defer func() {
		os.Stdout = origStdout
		os.Stderr = origStderr
	}()

	var outBuf bytes.Buffer
	var errBuf bytes.Buffer
	var wg sync.WaitGroup
	wg.Add(2)
	go func() {
		defer wg.Done()
		_, _ = io.Copy(&outBuf, stdoutR)
	}()
	go func() {
		defer wg.Done()
		_, _ = io.Copy(&errBuf, stderrR)
	}()

	root.SetArgs(args)
	err = root.Execute()

	_ = stdoutW.Close()
	_ = stderrW.Close()
	wg.Wait()

	return outBuf.String() + errBuf.String(), err
}

package cmd

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"runtime"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/workerstatus"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorktrees(t *testing.T) {
	if runtime.GOOS == "windows" {
		t.Skip("process-liveness startup cleanup test is unix-oriented")
	}

	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupWorkStartupCleanupProject(t)
	t.Setenv("DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE", "1h")

	attemptID := "20260515T010203-deadbeef"
	worktreePath := createRegisteredStartupWorktree(t, projectRoot, tempRoot, "ddx-startup-stale", attemptID)
	old := time.Now().Add(-2 * time.Hour).UTC()
	writeStartupRunState(t, projectRoot, "ddx-startup-stale", attemptID, worktreePath, deadProcessPID(t), old)
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(worktreePath, old, old))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "--json", "--once", "--project", projectRoot)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var res struct {
		NoReadyWork bool `json:"no_ready_work"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &res))
	assert.True(t, res.NoReadyWork)
	assert.NoFileExists(t, worktreePath)
	assert.NotContains(t, runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "worktree", "list", "--porcelain"), worktreePath)
}

func TestWorkStartup_PreservesLiveWorktrees(t *testing.T) {
	if runtime.GOOS == "windows" {
		t.Skip("process-liveness startup cleanup test is unix-oriented")
	}

	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupWorkStartupCleanupProject(t)
	t.Setenv("DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE", "1h")

	attemptID := "20260515T020304-feedface"
	worktreePath := createRegisteredStartupWorktree(t, projectRoot, tempRoot, "ddx-startup-live", attemptID)
	old := time.Now().Add(-2 * time.Hour).UTC()
	live := startLongLivedProcess(t)
	writeStartupRunState(t, projectRoot, "ddx-startup-live", attemptID, worktreePath, live.Process.Pid, old)
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(worktreePath, old, old))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "--json", "--once", "--project", projectRoot)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var res struct {
		NoReadyWork bool `json:"no_ready_work"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &res))
	assert.True(t, res.NoReadyWork)
	assert.DirExists(t, worktreePath)
	assert.Contains(t, runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "worktree", "list", "--porcelain"), worktreePath)
}

func TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorkerDirs(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, _ := setupWorkStartupCleanupProject(t)

	workersDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "workers")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(workersDir, 0o755))

	deadWorkerID := "agent-loop-dead"
	staleWorkerID := "agent-loop-stale"
	freshWorkerID := "agent-loop-fresh"
	now := time.Now().UTC()

	require.NoError(t, workerstatus.WriteLiveness(projectRoot, deadWorkerID, workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
		WorkerID:       deadWorkerID,
		ProjectRoot:    projectRoot,
		PID:            deadProcessPID(t),
		LastActivityAt: now,
	}))
	require.NoError(t, workerstatus.WriteLiveness(projectRoot, staleWorkerID, workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
		WorkerID:       staleWorkerID,
		ProjectRoot:    projectRoot,
		LastActivityAt: now.Add(-31 * time.Minute),
	}))
	require.NoError(t, workerstatus.WriteLiveness(projectRoot, freshWorkerID, workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
		WorkerID:       freshWorkerID,
		ProjectRoot:    projectRoot,
		LastActivityAt: now,
	}))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "--json", "--once", "--project", projectRoot)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var res struct {
		NoReadyWork bool `json:"no_ready_work"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &res))
	assert.True(t, res.NoReadyWork)
	assert.NoDirExists(t, filepath.Join(workersDir, deadWorkerID))
	assert.NoDirExists(t, filepath.Join(workersDir, staleWorkerID))
	assert.DirExists(t, filepath.Join(workersDir, freshWorkerID))
}

func TestExecutionsRetention_ArchivesOldEvidence(t *testing.T) {
	t.Run("archives old evidence under executions-archive", func(t *testing.T) {
		projectRoot := setupWorkStartupCleanupProjectRoot(t)
		t.Setenv(executionRetentionOverrideEnv, "7")

		oldTime := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -10).UTC()
		oldAttemptID := "20260101T000000-deadbeef"
		oldDir := setupWorkStartupEvidenceDir(t, projectRoot, oldAttemptID, oldTime)
		recentAttemptID := "20260501T000000-feedface"
		recentDir := setupWorkStartupEvidenceDir(t, projectRoot, recentAttemptID, time.Now().UTC())

		runner := newStartupHousekeepingRunner(projectRoot)
		report, err := runner.scan(context.Background(), true)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		archivedDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "executions-archive", "2026", "01", oldAttemptID)
		assert.NoDirExists(t, oldDir)
		assert.DirExists(t, archivedDir)
		assert.DirExists(t, recentDir)
		assert.Equal(t, int64(1), report.StaleExecutionDirs)
		assert.Equal(t, int64(1), report.ArchivedExecutionDirs)
		assert.Equal(t, int64(0), report.DeletedExecutionDirs)
	})

	t.Run("deletes old evidence when retain-days override is zero", func(t *testing.T) {
		projectRoot := setupWorkStartupCleanupProjectRoot(t)
		t.Setenv(executionRetentionOverrideEnv, "0")

		oldTime := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -10).UTC()
		oldAttemptID := "20260101T000000-cafebabe"
		oldDir := setupWorkStartupEvidenceDir(t, projectRoot, oldAttemptID, oldTime)

		runner := newStartupHousekeepingRunner(projectRoot)
		report, err := runner.scan(context.Background(), true)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		assert.NoDirExists(t, oldDir)
		assert.NoDirExists(t, filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "executions-archive", "2026", "01", oldAttemptID))
		assert.Equal(t, int64(1), report.StaleExecutionDirs)
		assert.Equal(t, int64(0), report.ArchivedExecutionDirs)
		assert.Equal(t, int64(1), report.DeletedExecutionDirs)
	})
}

func setupWorkStartupCleanupProject(t *testing.T) (string, string) {
	t.Helper()

	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "init", "-b", "main")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "add", "seed.txt")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed work startup cleanup repo")
	return projectRoot, tempRoot
}

func setupWorkStartupCleanupProjectRoot(t *testing.T) string {
	t.Helper()

	projectRoot := t.TempDir()
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"), 0o755))
	return projectRoot
}

func setupWorkStartupEvidenceDir(t *testing.T, projectRoot, attemptID string, mtime time.Time) string {
	t.Helper()

	dir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "executions", attemptID)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "result.json"), []byte(`{"status":"success"}`), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.Chtimes(dir, mtime, mtime))
	return dir
}

func setupWorkStartupCleanupProject(t *testing.T) (string, string) {
	t.Helper()

	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "init", "-b", "main")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "add", "seed.txt")
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed work startup cleanup repo")
	return projectRoot, tempRoot
}

func createRegisteredStartupWorktree(t *testing.T, projectRoot, tempRoot, beadID, attemptID string) string {
	t.Helper()

	worktreePath := filepath.Join(tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+beadID+"-"+attemptID)
	runCleanupCommandGit(t, projectRoot, "worktree", "add", "--detach", worktreePath, "HEAD")
	t.Cleanup(func() {
		_ = exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "worktree", "remove", "--force", worktreePath).Run()
	})
	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(worktreePath, agent.ExecutionCleanupMetadata{
		ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
		BeadID:       beadID,
		AttemptID:    attemptID,
		WorktreePath: worktreePath,
		Registered:   true,
	}))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(worktreePath, "scratch.txt"), []byte("payload\n"), 0o644))
	return worktreePath
}

func writeStartupRunState(t *testing.T, projectRoot, beadID, attemptID, worktreePath string, pid int, refreshedAt time.Time) {
	t.Helper()

	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteRunState(projectRoot, agent.RunState{
		BeadID:       beadID,
		AttemptID:    attemptID,
		StartedAt:    refreshedAt.Add(-5 * time.Minute),
		WorktreePath: worktreePath,
		PID:          pid,
		RefreshedAt:  refreshedAt,
		ExpiresAt:    refreshedAt.Add(5 * time.Minute),
	}))
}

func deadProcessPID(t *testing.T) int {
	t.Helper()

	cmd := exec.Command("sh", "-c", "exit 0")
	require.NoError(t, cmd.Start())
	pid := cmd.Process.Pid
	require.NoError(t, cmd.Wait())
	return pid
}

func startLongLivedProcess(t *testing.T) *exec.Cmd {
	t.Helper()

	cmd := exec.Command("sleep", "30")
	require.NoError(t, cmd.Start())
	t.Cleanup(func() {
		if cmd.Process == nil {
			return
		}
		_ = cmd.Process.Kill()
		_, _ = cmd.Process.Wait()
	})
	return cmd
}

Added shared startup housekeeping in [startup_housekeeping.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-887e2bd3-20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/cli/cmd/startup_housekeeping.go:1) and wired it into `ddx work`, `ddx try`, and `ddx doctor --apply` via [execute_loop_shared.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-887e2bd3-20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:1), [try.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-887e2bd3-20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/cli/cmd/try.go:1), and [doctor.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-887e2bd3-20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/cli/cmd/doctor.go:1). It now:
- reaps stale `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*` dirs when the PID is dead or `last_activity_at` is older than 30 minutes,
- archives old `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` dirs into `.ddx/executions-archive/<year>/<month>/`,
- deletes old execution dirs instead when `DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS=0`,
- reports stale worktree/worker/execution counts in `ddx doctor` dry runs.
I also ignored `.ddx/executions-archive/` in [.gitignore](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-887e2bd3-20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/.gitignore:1).

Coverage was added in [work_startup_cleanup_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-887e2bd3-20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/cli/cmd/work_startup_cleanup_test.go:1) and [doctor_housekeeping_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-887e2bd3-20260515T064010-6fbfbb43/cli/cmd/doctor_housekeeping_test.go:1), including `TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorkerDirs`, `TestExecutionsRetention_ArchivesOldEvidence`, and doctor dry-run vs `--apply`. Verification passed with `go test ./cmd` and `lefthook run pre-commit`. Commit: `631fec75bd9fface4f62e0f5969efc46115828ea` (`fix: reap stale worker and execution startup state [ddx-887e2bd3]`).
